input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Also attached is an image that shows an engineering drawing of the vehicle on the top accompanied by six CAD views of the vehicle on the bottom. The six CAD views each feature a different orientation of our design, so that 3D information about our design can be inferred. In the CAD views, the front hoop is highlighted in pink and the front hoop braces are highlighted in blue. The CAD views are provided to contextualize the engineering drawing, which has the same orientation as one of the six CAD views. All units displayed in the engineering drawing have units of mm. Based on the engineering drawing, does our design comply with rule F.6.3.4 specified in the FSAE rule document? Only use dimensions explicitly shown in the engineering drawing to answer the question. If a dimension is not explicitly shown, you can assume that it complies with the rules. First provide an explanation for your answer (begin it with 'Explanation:'). Then provide just a yes/no answer (begin it with 'Answer:') that summarizes your response.

retrieved_context:
RULE: f.6.3.4
            TITLE: the front hoop braces must be attached as near as possible to the top of the front hoop but

            CONTENT:
            rule F.6.3.4: The Front Hoop Braces must be attached as near as possible to the top of the Front Hoop but
F.6.3.4 The Front Hoop Braces must be attached as near as possible to the top of the Front Hoop but 
not more than 50 mm below the top-most surface of the Front Hoop.  See figure following

intermediate_steps.founded_objects: dimension arrow, dimension line, front hoop braces, front hoop

final_answer:
Explanation: Rule F.6.3.4 states that the Front Hoop Braces must not be attached more than 50 mm below the top-most surface of the Front Hoop. The engineering drawing explicitly shows a dimension labeled 'dimension arrow' indicating the vertical distance between the top of the Front Hoop and the attachment point of the Front Hoop Braces. This dimension is 37 mm. Since 37 mm is less than the maximum allowed distance of 50 mm, the design complies with this rule.
Answer: Yes
